deck_subtitle: Findings and recommendations for operational safety improvements
closing_statement: Execute the two operational changes and sustain strong PPE program to address all identified risks.
next_steps: Implement shift-change stagger protocol — owner TBC
Revise exit-check schedule to morning walk — owner TBC
Review PPE compliance metrics quarterly — owner TBC

items.title: Finding 1
items.text: Forklift near-misses in loading bay during shift changes

items.title: Finding 2
items.text: Fire-exit checks logged late — 30% in June

items.title: Finding 3
items.text: PPE compliance strong at 94%, above 90% target

body.heading: Forklift near-misses concentrated in loading bay during shift changes
points: Incident clustering observed during 2–4 PM shift transitions
Root cause: increased foot and vehicle traffic overlap during handoff
Hazard: inadequate physical separation during peak congestion times

body.heading: 30% of fire-exit checks logged late in June
points: Late documentation identified for exit checks; timing inconsistent
Current check process mid-shift, competing with operational demands
Risk: delayed response to potential exit blockages or signage failures

body.heading: PPE compliance at 94%, above 90% target
points: Strong sustained compliance demonstrates effective safety culture
Equipment availability and employee training reinforce this performance
Program warrants continued support and resourcing

body.heading: Loading bay near-miss incident patterns
body.caption: Spatial and temporal analysis of near-miss events, loading bay area

body.heading: Fire-exit check documentation timeline
body.caption: Check completion times in June showing late-documentation pattern

body.heading: Recommendations
points: Stagger shift-change times to reduce congestion and near-miss incidents in the loading bay
Relocate fire-exit checks to morning walk routines to ensure timely documentation
Maintain current PPE compliance program; performance above target demonstrates effectiveness
